Hashtable.java

00001 /* Hashtable.java -- a class providing a basic hashtable data structure,
00002    mapping Object --> Object

00032 independent module, the terms and conditions of the license of that
00033 module.  An independent module is a module which is not derived from
00034 or based on this library.  If you modify this library, you may extend

00038 
00039 package java.util;
00040 
00041 // NOTE: This implementation is very similar to that of HashMap. If you fix
00042 // a bug in here, chances are you should make a similar change to the HashMap
00043 // code.
00044 
00091 public class Hashtable
00092 {
00093     // WARNING: Hashtable is a CORE class in the bootstrap cycle. See the
00094     // comments in vm/reference/java/lang/Runtime for implications of this fact.
00095 
00099     private static final int DEFAULT_CAPACITY = 11;
00100 
00101     // Package visible for use by nested classes.
00103     static final int KEYS = 0;
00105     static final int VALUES = 1;
00107     static final int ENTRIES = 2;
00108 
00114     private int threshold;
00115 
00119     private static final float loadFactor = 0.75f;
00120 
00124     // Package visible for use by nested classes.
00125     HashEntry[] buckets;
00126 
00130     // Package visible for use by nested classes.
00131     int size;
00132 
00138     private static final class HashEntry
00139     {
00140         Object key;
00141         Object value;
00142     
00144         HashEntry next;
00145 
00151         HashEntry(Object key, Object value)
00152         {
00153             this.key = key;
00154             this.value = value;
00155         }
00156 
00163         public Object setValue(Object newVal)
00164         {
00165             Object prev_value;
00166         
00167             if (newVal == null)
00168                 throw new NullPointerException();
00169                 
00170             prev_value = value;
00171             value = newVal;
00172             
00173             return prev_value;
00174         }
00175     }
00176     
00191     private final class Enumerator implements Enumeration
00192     {
00196         final int type;
00198         int count = size;
00200         int idx = buckets.length;
00206         HashEntry next;
00207 
00212         Enumerator(int type)
00213         {
00214             this.type = type;
00215         }
00216 
00221         public boolean hasMoreElements()
00222         {
00223             return count > 0;
00224         }
00225 
00231         public Object nextElement()
00232         {
00233             if (count == 0)
00234                 throw new NoSuchElementException("Hashtable Enumerator");
00235       
00236             count--;
00237             HashEntry e = next;
00238 
00239             while (e == null)
00240                 e = buckets[--idx];
00241 
00242             next = e.next;
00243             return type == VALUES ? e.value : e.key;
00244          }
00245     } // class Enumerator
00246 
00251     public Hashtable()
00252     {
00253         this(DEFAULT_CAPACITY);
00254     }
00255 
00263     public Hashtable(int initialCapacity)
00264     {
00265         if (initialCapacity < 0)
00266             throw new IllegalArgumentException("Illegal Capacity: "
00267                 + initialCapacity);
00268 
00269         if (initialCapacity == 0)
00270             initialCapacity = 1;
00271             
00272         buckets = new HashEntry[initialCapacity];
00273         threshold = (int) (initialCapacity * loadFactor);
00274         size = 0;
00275     }
00276 
00281     public synchronized int size()
00282     {
00283         return size;
00284     }
00285     
00290     public synchronized boolean isEmpty()
00291     {
00292         return size == 0;
00293     }
00294 
00302     public Enumeration keys()
00303     {
00304         return new Enumerator(KEYS);
00305     }
00306 
00314     public Enumeration elements()
00315     {
00316         return new Enumerator(VALUES);
00317     }
00318 
00329     public synchronized boolean contains(Object value)
00330     {
00331         for (int i = buckets.length - 1; i >= 0; i--)
00332         {
00333             HashEntry e = buckets[i];
00334       
00335             while (e != null)
00336             {
00337                 if (value.equals(e.value))
00338                     return true;
00339       
00340                 e = e.next;
00341             }
00342         }
00343 
00344         // Must throw on null argument even if the table is empty
00345         if (value == null)
00346             throw new NullPointerException();
00347  
00348         return false;  
00349     }
00350 
00359     public synchronized boolean containsKey(Object key)
00360     {
00361         int idx = hash(key);
00362         HashEntry e = buckets[idx];
00363   
00364         while (e != null)
00365         {
00366             if (key.equals(e.key))
00367                 return true;
00368             e = e.next;
00369          }
00370        
00371          return false;
00372     }
00373 
00384     public synchronized Object get(Object key)
00385     {
00386         int idx = hash(key);
00387         HashEntry e = buckets[idx];
00388   
00389         while (e != null)
00390         {
00391             if (key.equals(e.key))
00392                 return e.value;
00393   
00394             e = e.next;
00395         }
00396       
00397         return null;
00398     }
00399 
00411     protected void rehash()
00412     {
00413         HashEntry[] oldBuckets = buckets;
00414 
00415         int newcapacity = (buckets.length * 2) + 1;
00416         threshold = (int) (newcapacity * loadFactor);
00417         buckets = new HashEntry[newcapacity];
00418 
00419         for (int i = oldBuckets.length - 1; i >= 0; i--)
00420         {
00421             HashEntry e = oldBuckets[i];
00422 
00423             while (e != null)
00424             {
00425                 int idx = hash(e.key);
00426                 HashEntry dest = buckets[idx];
00427 
00428                 if (dest != null)
00429                 {
00430                     while (dest.next != null)
00431                         dest = dest.next;
00432     
00433                     dest.next = e;
00434                 }
00435                 else
00436                 {
00437                     buckets[idx] = e;
00438                 }
00439 
00440                 HashEntry next = e.next;
00441                 e.next = null;
00442                 e = next;
00443             }
00444         }
00445     }
00446 
00459     public synchronized Object put(Object key, Object value)
00460     {
00461         int idx = hash(key);
00462         HashEntry e = buckets[idx];
00463 
00464         // Check if value is null since it is not permitted.
00465         if (value == null)
00466             throw new NullPointerException();
00467 
00468         while (e != null)
00469         {
00470             if (key.equals(e.key))
00471             {
00472                 // Bypass e.setValue, since we already know value is non-null.
00473                 Object r = e.value;
00474                 e.value = value;
00475                 return r;
00476             }
00477             else
00478             {
00479                 e = e.next;
00480             }
00481         }
00482 
00483         if (++size > threshold)
00484         {
00485             rehash();
00486             // Need a new hash value to suit the bigger table.
00487             idx = hash(key);
00488         }
00489 
00490         e = new HashEntry(key, value);
00491 
00492         e.next = buckets[idx];
00493         buckets[idx] = e;
00494 
00495         return null;
00496     }
00497 
00506     public synchronized Object remove(Object key)
00507     {
00508        int idx = hash(key);
00509         HashEntry e = buckets[idx];
00510         HashEntry last = null;
00511 
00512         while (e != null)
00513         {
00514             if (key.equals(e.key))
00515             {
00516                 if (last == null)
00517                     buckets[idx] = e.next;
00518                 else
00519                     last.next = e.next;
00520 
00521                 size--;
00522 
00523                 return e.value;
00524             }
00525     
00526             last = e;
00527             e = e.next;
00528         }
00529     
00530         return null;
00531     }
00532 
00536     public synchronized void clear()
00537     {
00538         if (size > 0)
00539         {
00540             for (int i = 0; i < buckets.length; i++)
00541                 buckets[i] = null;
00542 
00543             size = 0;
00544         }
00545     }
00546 
00557     public synchronized String toString()
00558     {
00559         // Since we are already synchronized, and entrySet().iterator()
00560         // would repeatedly re-lock/release the monitor, we directly use the
00561         // unsynchronized HashIterator instead.
00562         Enumeration entries = new Enumerator(ENTRIES);
00563         StringBuffer r = new StringBuffer("{");
00564 
00565         for (int pos = size; pos > 0; pos--)
00566         {
00567             r.append(entries.nextElement());
00568 
00569             if (pos > 1)
00570                 r.append(", ");
00571         }
00572 
00573         r.append("}");
00574         return r.toString();
00575     }
00576 
00585     private int hash(Object key)
00586     {
00587         // Note: Inline Math.abs here, for less method overhead, and to avoid
00588         // a bootstrap dependency, since Math relies on native methods.
00589         int hash = key.hashCode() % buckets.length;
00590         return hash < 0 ? -hash : hash;
00591     }
00592 } // class Hashtable
Generated on Mon Mar 14 2016 15:25:24 for @PACKAGE_NAME@ by  1.7.1
